rulings.Case Title: LONNA WAIS VS. ROBERT L FARMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Duces Tecum Seeking The Records Of William Shannahan And Req For Fees, Costs And Sanctions; Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 28. PLAINTIFF LONNA WAIS' Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Duces Tecum Seeking The Records Of William Shannahan And Request For Fees, Costs And Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan in its entirety. That recommendation is to quash the subpoena as to categories 1 through 5, deny the motion as to category 6, and limit category 7 to correspondence that Shannahan had with Carrier where Carrier acted in the capacity of a consultant or agent of Shannahan and require a privilege log for documents claimed to be privileged. Pro Tem Judge Kaplan's report and recommendation will be made available to the parties at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS FRANK ALIOTO, ALIOTO, FRANK ANGELO AS TRUSTEE OF THE ALIOTO'S Motion For Protective Order - Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The Court finds that the motion was not timely filed and that there was not sufficient meet and confer. The court orders that responses to these requests be served no later than February 16, 2012, unless the parties agree on a different date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA A. JAGIELLO VS. SUSAN ELISABETH CHRISTMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify John Mounier To Act As Counsel In This Matter
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 6. PLAINTIFF BARBARA JAGIELLO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify John Mounier To Act As Counsel In This Matter. Granted. Mr. Mounier's participation in the preparation of the retainer agreement and his work with and/or on behalf of Ms. Jagiello concerning Ms. Jagiello's proposed representation of Ms. Christman requires that Mr. Mounier be disqualified even in the absence of any showing that Ms. Jagiello communicated information in confidence to Mr. Mounier which Mr. Mounier agreed to, or should have understood was, not to be disclosed to Ms. Christman. rulings.Case Title: RED EAGLE AIR CORP., A UTAH CORPORATION VS. MR AVIATION, LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 11. PLAINTIFF RED EAGLE AIR CORP., A UTAH CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. As as matter of law, the liquidated damages provision was not reasonable at the time the lease was signed because: a) the amount of liquidated damages does not bear any reasonable relationship to the amount of the likely actual damages; b) the amount of liquidated damages has an inverse relationship to the likely actual damages insofar as the timing of the breach is concerned (i.e. the earlier the breach and the less likely the amount of the actual damages, the provision imposes a greater amount of liquidated damages and vice-versa) and c) the provision vitiates the important and longstanding principle of California law that the non-breaching party has a duty to mitigate its damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: REGINA M CARLIN VS. PATRICK TOBIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF REGINA CARLIN Ntc Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses - Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied in part and granted in part. Sanctions are awarded against counsel Katrin Cusack in the amount of $5000.00. The motion as to compelling or deeming admitted the Request for Admissions is denied. The Court finds that there was substantial compliance with the CCP and the prior Court order. Motion as to the Form Interrogatories is denied as to Interrogatory No. 17.1 and granted as to Interrogatories 12.4 and 14.2. The prior Court order required a statement of diligence and efforts of search when no answer was known. No such statement was provided. Motion is granted as to the Requests for Production of Documents. A declaration is not a proper response and the prior written responses are not compliant with CCP sections 2031.210-2031.230. There is not statement delineating whether production will be made or if not, the reason for the inability. Finally, the lack of verifications for the original responses and the supplemental responses are in violation of the CCP and the prior court order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT SMART & FINAL STORES LLC (ERRONEOUSLY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) And Special Interrogatories (Set One); Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted in part and denied in part. The motion with regard to the special interrogatories is granted as to numbers 22, 30 and 41-46 and denied as to the remaining interrogatories (6, 20, 21, 23, 24, 28, 31 and 32). The Court awards sanctions against Plaintiff's counsel, Gary Nelson in the amount of $500.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ALLSTATE INSURANCE VS. PETER SUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Correct Arbitration Award And Confirm Award As Corrected And Req. For Statement Of Decision
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 16. PETITIONER ALLSTATE INSURANCE'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Correct Arbitration Award And Confirm Award As Corrected And Request For Statement Of Decision. Granted in part. The award is corrected to reduce the amount of costs awarded to Mr. Sung by $4875 and confirmed as corrected. The Arbitrator exceeded his authority in awarding arbitration expenses which the parties contractually agreed would be shared equally. The Arbitrator did not exceed his authority in awarding other costs, which did not include attorney's fees or fees paid to medical or other expert witnesses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RAIF B. DENKTAS VS. ROXUL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery And To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 30. DEFENDANTS ROXUL, LLC AND LUXOR CABS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan in its entirety. That recommendation is to a) grant the motion to compel as to the special and form interrogatories and document requests and require the plaintiff to serve objection-free responses and to produce the requested documents within twenty days, b) deny the motion to have facts deemed admitted, but to require plaintiff to serve objection-free responses within twenty days; and c) deny the request for sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Kaplan's report and recommendation will be made available to the parties at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EBONY BRIDEWELL-SLEDGE et al VS. BLUS CROSS OF CALIFORNIA, A CA CORPORATION et al***TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue And Request For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Against Plaintiff'S Counsel; Points And Authorities, Declarations Of Jill A Porcaro And Denise Held In Support; Appendix Of Non Published Federal Authority
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 21. DEFENDANT BLUE CROSS OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION AND ANTHEM BLUE CROSS LIFE AND HEALTH INSURANCE'S Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue And Request For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Against Plaintiff'S Counsel. Granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 396(b) and 397(a) and Government Code 12965(b). Judge Sohigian did not rule on the merits of a motion invoking section 12965(b) and thus this motion is not seeking reconsideration. Defendants have shown that venue is improper in this county per section 12965(b). All requests for attorney's fees are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUSY BUDIMAN VS. PATRICIA LURIE et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 26, 2012, Line 25. DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action and without leave to amend as to the seventh, ninth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action. The second cause of action is deficient because it does not allege violation of a public policy grounded in a state or federal constitutional provision or statute. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of the seventh, ninth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action.
